subtopics.title: Living and non-living things
subtopics.content: Observe, compare and classify living and non-living things.

subtopics.title: Diversity of plants and animals
subtopics.content: Classify plants and animals based on observable similarities and differences.

subtopics.title: Diversity of materials
subtopics.content: Classify materials by properties and relate properties to uses.

subtopics.title: Life cycles of plants and animals
subtopics.content: Compare stages of life cycles and explain continuity of life.

subtopics.title: Matter, water and changes of state
subtopics.content: Describe states of matter, changes of state and the water cycle.

subtopics.title: Reproduction in plants and animals
subtopics.content: Explain reproduction as a process that ensures continuity of species.

subtopics.title: Plant parts and functions
subtopics.content: Relate plant parts to functions such as transport, reproduction and making food.

subtopics.title: Human respiratory, circulatory and digestive systems
subtopics.content: Describe major organs, functions and how body systems work together.

subtopics.title: Electrical systems
subtopics.content: Build and explain simple electrical circuits and component functions.

subtopics.title: Forces
subtopics.content: Identify forces and explain their effects on motion, shape and position.

subtopics.title: Interactions within the environment
subtopics.content: Explain food chains, adaptations and effects of changes in an environment.

subtopics.title: Light
subtopics.content: Explain sources of light, shadows, reflection and how light travels.

subtopics.title: Heat
subtopics.content: Explain temperature, heat transfer, expansion/contraction and everyday applications.

subtopics.title: Photosynthesis
subtopics.content: Explain the role of light, water and carbon dioxide in food-making by plants.

subtopics.title: Energy conversion
subtopics.content: Identify energy forms and describe how energy changes from one form to another.

noteCards.title: Claim, evidence, reason
noteCards.body: Open-ended PSLE Science answers should state the claim, point to evidence in the question, then explain the science reason.
noteCards.workedExample: Claim: The plant will grow less well. Evidence: It receives less light. Reason: Plants need light for photosynthesis to make food.
noteCards.commonMistake: Giving a fact without linking it to the question evidence.

quizCards.prompt: A plant receives less light but the same amount of water. Which variable was changed?
quizCards.explanation: Only light changed; water was kept constant.
